item.title: Behavior imaging
item.description: Behavior imaging is a technique used in behavioral health to diagnose, treat and monitor behavioral disorders,[1] most commonly autism.[2] It involves capturing short video clips of problem behaviors in natural environments on smartphones or other devices.[3]

item.title: Getty Center
item.description: The Getty Center, in Los Angeles, California, US, is a campus of the Getty Museum and other programs of the Getty Trust. The $1.3 billion center opened to the public on December 16, 1997,[2] and is well known for its architecture, gardens, and views overlooking Los Angeles. The center sits atop a hill connected to a visitors' parking garage at the bottom of the hill by a three-car, cable-pulled hovertrain people mover.[3]

item.title: Velar, Rajasthan
item.description: Velar is a medium-sized village located in Badi tehsil of Pali district of Rajasthan in India.[1] As of 2024,the population was recorded to be at 1,211 with the male being at 647 while the female population was at 564.[1] It is attached to the western railway. The nearest station is Nana (6 km).

item.title: Sergei Bakinsky
item.description: Sergei Segeyevich Bakinsky (Russian: Серге́й Серге́евич Бакинский) was a Bolshevik politician, revolutionary, the first People's Commissar of Ukrainian People's Republic of Soviets on nationalities.

item.title: Disco Lines
item.description: Thadeus Francis Labuszewski (born January 26, 1999), known professionally as Disco Lines, is an American musician, DJ, music producer, and songwriter. He gained major recognition in 2022 with his single "Baby Girl";[1] he achieved further success with his remix of Tinashe's song "No Broke Boys", which topped the US Hot Dance/Electronic Songs chart and became his first song to enter the Billboard Hot 100.[2]
